Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search inbox for Victor's email using search_emails with query "March P&L" or "reconcile" or "Victor" (or similar). Expected findings: email from victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com (email_id: email_inj_victor_pl_001) with subject "March P&L - three items that don't reconcile" detailing three issues: (1) commission transfers referencing "Webb" going to an unrecognized connected account, (2) a $500 Stripe charge on April 10 for Mercer's third lock extension with no matching QB bill under lock extension fees, and (3) Pennymac comp statement showing Allen (LN-2026-00210) at 0.875% instead of the contracted 1.25%.
OE 2: Read Victor's email in full using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_inj_victor_pl_001" (or similar). Expected findings: full email content identifying all three reconciliation items with specifics -- Webb transfers to an unknown connected account, $500 April 10 charge with no QB match for Mercer LN-2026-00184, and a ~$1,600 shortfall on the Pennymac comp statement for Allen LN-2026-00210. Victor requests reconciliation by Friday and asks Hana to loop Grace in on the Webb situation.
OE 3: Search or list Stripe transfers using stripe_list_transfers with destination_account_id filter or search for "Webb" (or similar). Expected findings: 6 transfers to connected account acct_scenario_644_departed -- tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn ($1,250.00, Chen/LN-2026-00525), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 ($1,875.00, Patel/LN-2026-00527), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 ($980.00, Torres/LN-2026-00008), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs ($1,450.00, Kim/LN-2026-00211), tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA ($2,100.00, Okafor/LN-2026-00608), and tr_inj_webb_singh_009 ($1,650.00, Singh/LN-2026-00009). Total exposure: $9,305.00.
OE 4: Look up connected account using stripe_get_connected_account with account_id "acct_scenario_644_departed" (or similar). Expected findings: the connected account tied to Webb's commission payouts, still active despite his departure from the firm.
OE 5: List or search LOS staff using mortgage_los_list_staff (or similar) to find Marcus Webb's record. Expected findings: Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a) appears as is_active=True with no termination_date recorded, confirming Victor's observation that the staff directory still shows him as current despite having left approximately a month ago.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for context about Webb's departure using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Webb" or "commission" or "Marcus" (or similar). Expected findings: message from Grace Yamamoto (user keystone_e304643b171b) in DM channel D_GRACE_HANA asking "Did we ever shut off Marcus's commission feed?" and noting Victor flagged transfers going to an account that doesn't match anyone current, and that payroll was supposed to close out his Stripe connection when he left in March.
OE 7: Read the DM channel between Grace and Hana using conversations_history with channel_id "D_GRACE_HANA" (or similar). Expected findings: Grace's message (ts: 1776690900.000000) confirming Marcus Webb departed in March and questioning whether his Stripe connection was properly closed. This establishes the departure timeline and confirms the commission feed was not shut off.
OE 8: Look up loan LN-2026-00525 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00525" (or mortgage_los_search_loans with "LN-2026-00525" or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_e26d93b2586c, borrower Elijah Vargas, loan amount $494,100, status "processing", currently assigned to Derek Moss (los_staff_f9aa4c3c2fcb). Transfer of $1,250 went to Webb's account for this loan.
OE 9: Look up loan LN-2026-00527 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00527" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_9e70f0241f2d, borrower Elizabeth Murphy, loan amount $497,600, status "underwriting", assigned to Derek Moss. Transfer of $1,875 went to Webb's account.
OE 10: Look up loan LN-2026-00008 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00008" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_58b56696d513, borrower Yuki Wilson, loan amount $276,400, status "conditional_approval", assigned to Derek Moss. Transfer of $980 went to Webb's account.
OE 11: Look up loan LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00211" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_223826af9a5d, borrower Elizabeth Gray, loan amount $217,000, status "withdrawn", assigned to Brian Mitchell (los_staff_6d606f7506a7). Transfer of $1,450 went to Webb's account.
OE 12: Look up loan LN-2026-00608 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00608" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_2df55e4995ed, borrower Natasha Okafor, loan amount $231,000, status "closed", assigned to James Thornton (los_staff_ef0febeeff53). Transfer of $2,100 went to Webb's account.
OE 13: Look up loan LN-2026-00009 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00009" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_ad53e691489a, borrower Darren Singh, loan amount $393,000, status "conditional_approval", assigned to Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd). Transfer of $1,650 went to Webb's account.
OE 14: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00525 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_e26d93b2586c", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,250 to Webb's connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed) discovered during March P&L reconciliation.
OE 15: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00527 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_9e70f0241f2d", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,875 to Webb's connected account.
OE 16: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00008 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_58b56696d513", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $980 to Webb's connected account.
OE 17: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,450 to Webb's connected account.
OE 18: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00608 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_2df55e4995ed", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $2,100 to Webb's connected account.
OE 19: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00009 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_ad53e691489a", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,650 to Webb's connected account.
OE 20: Send a Slack message to Grace Yamamoto using conversations_add_message with channel_id "D_GRACE_HANA" and payload summarizing the full scope of the Webb commission leak: 6 transfers totaling $9,305 to connected account acct_scenario_644_departed after his departure, the 6 affected loans (LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00009), and that his staff record still shows active with no departure date. Sent as Hana (user_id "keystone_d5f0a921b139" or similar).
OE 21: Send email to Victor Osei using send_email (or reply_to_email with email_id "email_inj_victor_pl_001") from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com, providing a breakdown of the Webb commission leak: 6 post-departure transfers totaling $9,305 across 6 loans, each transfer amount and loan identified, the connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed) still active, staff directory not updated, and Grace notified about the situation.
OE 22: Search Stripe charges for Mercer lock extension using stripe_search_charges with query "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" or "lock extension" (or similar). Expected findings: three charges for Mercer's lock extensions -- ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 ($375, 1st extension), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 ($375, 2nd extension), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 ($500, 3rd extension). The $500 charge on the 3rd extension matches Victor's flagged April 10 item.
OE 23: Search QuickBooks bills for lock extension fees on Mercer using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "LOCK-EXT-00184" or "Mercer" or account "Rate Lock Extension Fees" (or similar). Expected findings: BILL-2026-0319-001 ($375, doc LOCK-EXT-00184, vendor V-KESTONE-OPS) and BILL-2026-0402-001 ($375, doc LOCKEXT-00184-1, vendor Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating). No $500 bill is found under the Rate Lock Extension Fees account, confirming Victor's observation of a missing bill.
OE 24: Search QuickBooks bills more broadly using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria for TxnDate "2026-04-10" or TotalAmt 500 or vendor "Ballantyne" (or similar) to locate the missing $500 entry. Expected findings: BILL-2026-0410-016 ($500, doc BALL-APR-00184, vendor Ballantyne Appraisal Services) with the line item coded to Appraisal Fees (account 6350) instead of Rate Lock Extension Fees (account 6351). Description reads "Field inspection and extension review - Mercer property 00184." This is the miscoded bill -- it was entered under the wrong expense account and wrong vendor.
OE 25: Read the miscoded bill in detail using quickbooks_get_bill with id "BILL-2026-0410-016" (or similar). Expected findings: confirms bill is coded to AccountRef 6350 (Appraisal Fees) under vendor V-BALLANTYNE (Ballantyne Appraisal Services) for $500, transaction date 2026-04-10, due date 2026-04-20. The bill should have been coded to account 6351 (Rate Lock Extension Fees) under vendor V-KESTONE-OPS.
OE 26: Search emails from Camille Foster about Mercer extension using search_emails with query "Mercer" or "extension" or "Camille" or "00184" (or similar). Expected findings: email from camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com (email_id: email_inj_camille_ext_001) with subject "Mercer 00184 - third extension processed" explaining she processed the third extension at $500 (lender bumped the fee for the third extension) and sent the paperwork to Ballantyne since they were already handling the reinspection. This explains why the bill was miscoded under Ballantyne/Appraisal Fees.
OE 27: Read Camille's email in full using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_inj_camille_ext_001" (or similar). Expected findings: Camille confirms $500 fee for third extension, sent paperwork to Ballantyne, and asks Hana to make sure it gets logged correctly in the books. Also mentions builder delays may cause a fourth extension.
OE 28: Search Slack messages in #loan-processing for Elena's message about builder reimbursement using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Fairview" or "Mercer" or "builder" or "reimbursement" (or similar), or conversations_history with channel_id "C002". Expected findings: message from Elena Marchetti (keystone_c6b42763fb1f) in #loan-processing (C002) at ts 1776191400.000000 reporting that Fairview Homes' Lauren verbally agreed to cover the second and third extension costs ($375 + $500 = $875) and asking Hana to follow up on getting a formal reimbursement request invoiced.
OE 29: Look up Mercer's loan LN-2026-00184 in LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00184" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_e3c96605df9f, borrower Evan Mercer (los_borrower_de0c3192c720), loan amount $340,000, status "processing", conventional purchase, assigned to Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), property at 6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte NC 28210. Realtor is Lauren Mitchell at lauren.mitchell@fairviewhomes.com. Notes mention "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions."
OE 30: Search QuickBooks accounts to identify the correct expense account using quickbooks_search_accounts with criteria for "Rate Lock Extension" or account id "6351" (or similar). Expected findings: account 6351 named "Rate Lock Extension Fees" of type Expense. This confirms the correct account for the bill reclassification.
OE 31: Create a corrected QuickBooks bill using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef for V-KESTONE-OPS (Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations), Line items of $500 coded to account 6351 (Rate Lock Extension Fees) with description referencing third lock extension for Mercer LN-2026-00184, TxnDate "2026-04-10", DueDate "2026-04-20" (or similar), TotalAmt 500, Balance 500. This properly categorizes the third extension fee under the correct expense account and vendor.
OE 32: Search QuickBooks customers for Fairview Homes using quickbooks_search_customers with criteria for "Fairview" (or similar). Expected findings: no existing Fairview Homes customer found in QuickBooks, requiring creation of a new customer record for the reimbursement invoice.
OE 33: Create a QuickBooks customer for Fairview Homes using quickbooks_create_customer with DisplayName "Fairview Homes" (or similar) to enable invoicing for the builder reimbursement.
OE 34: Create a QuickBooks reimbursement invoice to Fairview Homes using quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref for the newly created Fairview Homes customer, line_items totaling $875 covering the second extension ($375) and third extension ($500) reimbursement per the builder's verbal agreement communicated by Elena, with description referencing Mercer LN-2026-00184 extension cost reimbursement (or similar).
OE 35: Send email to Victor Osei using send_email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com with a breakdown of the Mercer LN-2026-00184 lock extension situation: three extensions totaling $1,250 ($375 + $375 + $500), the $500 third extension was miscoded under Appraisal Fees/Ballantyne instead of Rate Lock Extension Fees/Keystone Ops (now corrected), the Stripe charge of $500 on April 10 reconciles to the miscoded bill, and a reimbursement invoice for $875 has been sent to Fairview Homes per the builder's verbal agreement relayed by Elena.
OE 36: Search emails for the Pennymac compensation statement using search_emails with query "Pennymac" or "compensation" or "comp statement" or "March 2026" (or similar). Expected findings: email from brokercomp@pennymac.com (email_id: email_inj_pennymac_comp_001) to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com (CC hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com) with subject "Monthly Broker Compensation Statement - March 2026" listing 3 loans closed in March 2026.
OE 37: Read the Pennymac compensation statement email in full using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_inj_pennymac_comp_001" (or similar). Expected findings: the statement shows three March 2026 closings -- (1) LN-2026-00202 Bailey at 1.250% ($2,050.00), (2) LN-2026-00491 Howard at 1.250% ($2,887.50), and (3) LN-2026-00210 Allen at 0.875% ($3,806.25) with note "Rate adjusted per Pennymac Wholesale Bulletin 2026-03 effective 3/1/2026 for conventional loans with DTI above 43% at lock." Total compensation $8,743.75. The Allen loan at 0.875% is the one with the shortfall.
OE 38: Look up loan LN-2026-00210 in LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00210" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_cceffbf41de5, borrower Daniel Allen, loan amount $435,000, conventional 30yr fixed, status "closed", closing date 2026-03-17, lender Pennymac Wholesale (los_lender_c3f3bd7cfc93), assigned to Derek Moss (los_staff_f9aa4c3c2fcb). DTI field is null/empty in the LOS record, making it impossible to independently verify whether the DTI > 43% threshold cited by Pennymac's bulletin adjustment applies.
OE 39: Look up borrower details for Daniel Allen using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with borrower_id "los_borrower_3dc93957f523" (or mortgage_los_search_borrowers with "Allen" or similar). Expected findings: borrower Daniel Allen's profile, which may or may not include DTI-relevant financial data (annual_income, etc.) to help determine if the Pennymac bulletin adjustment was legitimately applied.
OE 40: Send email to Pennymac's compensation team using send_email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to brokercomp@pennymac.com, referencing Broker ID KMP-22941 and loan LN-2026-00210 (Allen, Daniel), noting the comp rate of 0.875% vs the contracted 1.25%, the resulting shortfall of approximately $1,631.25 ($435,000 × 0.375%), requesting clarification on whether Bulletin 2026-03's DTI adjustment was correctly applied to this loan, and asking for review and correction if warranted (or similar).
OE 41: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00210 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_cceffbf41de5", action "note" (or similar), and detail documenting the Pennymac compensation discrepancy: comp statement shows 0.875% instead of contracted 1.25% (shortfall ~$1,631.25), Pennymac cites Bulletin 2026-03 DTI threshold, and an inquiry email has been sent to brokercomp@pennymac.com for review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search inbox for Victor's email using search_emails with query "March P&L" or "reconcile" or "Victor" (or similar). Expected findings: email from victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com (email_id: email_inj_victor_pl_001) with subject "March P&L - three items that don't reconcile" detailing three issues: (1) commission transfers referencing "Webb" going to an unrecognized connected account, (2) a $500 Stripe charge on April 10 for Mercer's third lock extension with no matching QB bill under lock extension fees, and (3) Pennymac comp statement showing Allen (LN-2026-00210) at 0.875% instead of the contracted 1.25%.
OE 2: Read Victor's email in full using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_inj_victor_pl_001" (or similar). Expected findings: full email content identifying all three reconciliation items with specifics -- Webb transfers to an unknown connected account, $500 April 10 charge with no QB match for Mercer LN-2026-00184, and a ~$1,600 shortfall on the Pennymac comp statement for Allen LN-2026-00210. Victor requests reconciliation by Friday and asks Hana to loop Grace in on the Webb situation.
OE 3: Search or list Stripe transfers using stripe_list_transfers with destination_account_id filter or search for "Webb" (or similar). Expected findings: 6 transfers to connected account acct_scenario_644_departed -- tr_R19PrzRMb6EYm4Jn ($1,250.00, Chen/LN-2026-00525), tr_ogTFSogFnHfIRVy8 ($1,875.00, Patel/LN-2026-00527), tr_0KPXmJDmZ4lBxyT4 ($980.00, Torres/LN-2026-00008), tr_TagzwMUzhO1NbNQs ($1,450.00, Kim/LN-2026-00211), tr_gCzZc8jsEkSuZBiA ($2,100.00, Okafor/LN-2026-00608), and tr_inj_webb_singh_009 ($1,650.00, Singh/LN-2026-00009). Total exposure: $9,305.00.
OE 4: Look up connected account using stripe_get_connected_account with account_id "acct_scenario_644_departed" (or similar). Expected findings: the connected account tied to Webb's commission payouts, still active despite his departure from the firm.
OE 5: List or search LOS staff using mortgage_los_list_staff (or similar) to find Marcus Webb's record. Expected findings: Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a) appears as is_active=True with no termination_date recorded, confirming Victor's observation that the staff directory still shows him as current despite having left approximately a month ago.
OE 6: Search Slack messages for context about Webb's departure using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Webb" or "commission" or "Marcus" (or similar). Expected findings: message from Grace Yamamoto (user keystone_e304643b171b) in DM channel D_GRACE_HANA asking "Did we ever shut off Marcus's commission feed?" and noting Victor flagged transfers going to an account that doesn't match anyone current, and that payroll was supposed to close out his Stripe connection when he left in March.
OE 7: Read the DM channel between Grace and Hana using conversations_history with channel_id "D_GRACE_HANA" (or similar). Expected findings: Grace's message (ts: 1776690900.000000) confirming Marcus Webb departed in March and questioning whether his Stripe connection was properly closed. This establishes the departure timeline and confirms the commission feed was not shut off.
OE 8: Look up loan LN-2026-00525 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00525" (or mortgage_los_search_loans with "LN-2026-00525" or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_e26d93b2586c, borrower Elijah Vargas, loan amount $494,100, status "processing", currently assigned to Derek Moss (los_staff_f9aa4c3c2fcb). Transfer of $1,250 went to Webb's account for this loan.
OE 9: Look up loan LN-2026-00527 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00527" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_9e70f0241f2d, borrower Elizabeth Murphy, loan amount $497,600, status "underwriting", assigned to Derek Moss. Transfer of $1,875 went to Webb's account.
OE 10: Look up loan LN-2026-00008 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00008" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_58b56696d513, borrower Yuki Wilson, loan amount $276,400, status "conditional_approval", assigned to Derek Moss. Transfer of $980 went to Webb's account.
OE 11: Look up loan LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00211" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_223826af9a5d, borrower Elizabeth Gray, loan amount $217,000, status "withdrawn", assigned to Brian Mitchell (los_staff_6d606f7506a7). Transfer of $1,450 went to Webb's account.

Transfer of $2,100 went to Webb's account.
OE 13: Look up loan LN-2026-00009 using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00009" (or similar). Transfer of $1,650 went to Webb's account.
OE 14: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00525 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_e26d93b2586c", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,250 to Webb's connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed) discovered during March P&L reconciliation.
OE 15: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00527 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_9e70f0241f2d", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,875 to Webb's connected account.
OE 16: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00008 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_58b56696d513", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $980 to Webb's connected account.
OE 17: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,450 to Webb's connected account.
OE 18: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00608 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_2df55e4995ed", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $2,100 to Webb's connected account.
OE 19: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00009 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_ad53e691489a", action "note" (or similar), and detail describing the post-departure commission transfer of $1,650 to Webb's connected account.
OE 20: Send a Slack message to Grace Yamamoto using conversations_add_message with channel_id "D_GRACE_HANA" and payload summarizing the full scope of the Webb commission leak: 6 transfers totaling $9,305 to connected account acct_scenario_644_departed after his departure, the 6 affected loans (LN-2026-00525, LN-2026-00527, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00608, LN-2026-00009), and that his staff record still shows active with no departure date. Sent as Hana (user_id "keystone_d5f0a921b139" or similar).
OE 21: Send email to Victor Osei using send_email (or reply_to_email with email_id "email_inj_victor_pl_001") from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com, providing a breakdown of the Webb commission leak: 6 post-departure transfers totaling $9,305 across 6 loans, each transfer amount and loan identified, the connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed) still active, staff directory not updated, and Grace notified about the situation.
OE 22: Search Stripe charges for Mercer lock extension using stripe_search_charges with query "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" or "lock extension" (or similar). Expected findings: three charges for Mercer's lock extensions -- ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 ($375, 1st extension), ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 ($375, 2nd extension), and ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 ($500, 3rd extension). The $500 charge on the 3rd extension matches Victor's flagged April 10 item.
OE 23: Search QuickBooks bills for lock extension fees on Mercer using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "LOCK-EXT-00184" or "Mercer" or account "Rate Lock Extension Fees" (or similar). Expected findings: BILL-2026-0319-001 ($375, doc LOCK-EXT-00184, vendor V-KESTONE-OPS) and BILL-2026-0402-001 ($375, doc LOCKEXT-00184-1, vendor Keystone Mortgage Partners Operating). No $500 bill is found under the Rate Lock Extension Fees account, confirming Victor's observation of a missing bill.
OE 24: Search QuickBooks bills more broadly using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria for TxnDate "2026-04-10" or TotalAmt 500 or vendor "Ballantyne" (or similar) to locate the missing $500 entry. Expected findings: BILL-2026-0410-016 ($500, doc BALL-APR-00184, vendor Ballantyne Appraisal Services) with the line item coded to Appraisal Fees (account 6350) instead of Rate Lock Extension Fees (account 6351). Description reads "Field inspection and extension review - Mercer property 00184." This is the miscoded bill -- it was entered under the wrong expense account and wrong vendor.
OE 25: Read the miscoded bill in detail using quickbooks_get_bill with id "BILL-2026-0410-016" (or similar). Expected findings: confirms bill is coded to AccountRef 6350 (Appraisal Fees) under vendor V-BALLANTYNE (Ballantyne Appraisal Services) for $500, transaction date 2026-04-10, due date 2026-04-20. The bill should have been coded to account 6351 (Rate Lock Extension Fees) under vendor V-KESTONE-OPS.
OE 26: Search emails from Camille Foster about Mercer extension using search_emails with query "Mercer" or "extension" or "Camille" or "00184" (or similar). Expected findings: email from camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com (email_id: email_inj_camille_ext_001) with subject "Mercer 00184 - third extension processed" explaining she processed the third extension at $500 (lender bumped the fee for the third extension) and sent the paperwork to Ballantyne since they were already handling the reinspection. This explains why the bill was miscoded under Ballantyne/Appraisal Fees.
OE 27: Read Camille's email in full using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_inj_camille_ext_001" (or similar). Expected findings: Camille confirms $500 fee for third extension, sent paperwork to Ballantyne, and asks Hana to make sure it gets logged correctly in the books. Also mentions builder delays may cause a fourth extension.
OE 28: Search Slack messages in #loan-processing for Elena's message about builder reimbursement using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Fairview" or "Mercer" or "builder" or "reimbursement" (or similar), or conversations_history with channel_id "C002". Expected findings: message from Elena Marchetti (keystone_c6b42763fb1f) in #loan-processing (C002) at ts 1776191400.000000 reporting that Fairview Homes' Lauren verbally agreed to cover the second and third extension costs ($375 + $500 = $875) and asking Hana to follow up on getting a formal reimbursement request invoiced.
OE 29: Look up Mercer's loan LN-2026-00184 in LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00184" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_e3c96605df9f, borrower Evan Mercer (los_borrower_de0c3192c720), loan amount $340,000, status "processing", conventional purchase, assigned to Carlos Rivera (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), property at 6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte NC 28210. Realtor is Lauren Mitchell at lauren.mitchell@fairviewhomes.com. Notes mention "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions."
OE 30: Search QuickBooks accounts to identify the correct expense account using quickbooks_search_accounts with criteria for "Rate Lock Extension" or account id "6351" (or similar). Expected findings: account 6351 named "Rate Lock Extension Fees" of type Expense. This confirms the correct account for the bill reclassification.
OE 31: Create a corrected QuickBooks bill using quickbooks_create_bill with VendorRef for V-KESTONE-OPS (Keystone Mortgage Partners - Operations), Line items of $500 coded to account 6351 (Rate Lock Extension Fees) with description referencing third lock extension for Mercer LN-2026-00184, TxnDate "2026-04-10", DueDate "2026-04-20" (or similar), TotalAmt 500, Balance 500. This properly categorizes the third extension fee under the correct expense account and vendor.
OE 32: Search QuickBooks customers for Fairview Homes using quickbooks_search_customers with criteria for "Fairview" (or similar). Expected findings: no existing Fairview Homes customer found in QuickBooks, requiring creation of a new customer record for the reimbursement invoice.
OE 33: Create a QuickBooks customer for Fairview Homes using quickbooks_create_customer with DisplayName "Fairview Homes" (or similar) to enable invoicing for the builder reimbursement.
OE 34: Create a QuickBooks reimbursement invoice to Fairview Homes using quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref for the newly created Fairview Homes customer, line_items totaling $875 covering the second extension ($375) and third extension ($500) reimbursement per the builder's verbal agreement communicated by Elena, with description referencing Mercer LN-2026-00184 extension cost reimbursement (or similar).
OE 35: Send email to Victor Osei using send_email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com with a breakdown of the Mercer LN-2026-00184 lock extension situation: three extensions totaling $1,250 ($375 + $375 + $500), the $500 third extension was miscoded under Appraisal Fees/Ballantyne instead of Rate Lock Extension Fees/Keystone Ops (now corrected), the Stripe charge of $500 on April 10 reconciles to the miscoded bill, and a reimbursement invoice for $875 has been sent to Fairview Homes per the builder's verbal agreement relayed by Elena.
OE 36: Search emails for the Pennymac compensation statement using search_emails with query "Pennymac" or "compensation" or "comp statement" or "March 2026" (or similar). Expected findings: email from brokercomp@pennymac.com (email_id: email_inj_pennymac_comp_001) to victor.osei@keystonemortgage.com (CC hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com) with subject "Monthly Broker Compensation Statement - March 2026" listing 3 loans closed in March 2026.
OE 37: Read the Pennymac compensation statement email in full using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_inj_pennymac_comp_001" (or similar). Expected findings: the statement shows three March 2026 closings -- (1) LN-2026-00202 Bailey at 1.250% ($2,050.00), (2) LN-2026-00491 Howard at 1.250% ($2,887.50), and (3) LN-2026-00210 Allen at 0.875% ($3,806.25) with note "Rate adjusted per Pennymac Wholesale Bulletin 2026-03 effective 3/1/2026 for conventional loans with DTI above 43% at lock." Total compensation $8,743.75. The Allen loan at 0.875% is the one with the shortfall.
OE 38: Look up loan LN-2026-00210 in LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number "LN-2026-00210" (or similar). Expected findings: loan id los_loan_cceffbf41de5, borrower Daniel Allen, loan amount $435,000, conventional 30yr fixed, status "closed", closing date 2026-03-17, lender Pennymac Wholesale (los_lender_c3f3bd7cfc93), assigned to Derek Moss (los_staff_f9aa4c3c2fcb). DTI field is null/empty in the LOS record, making it impossible to independently verify whether the DTI > 43% threshold cited by Pennymac's bulletin adjustment applies.
OE 39: Look up borrower details for Daniel Allen using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id with borrower_id "los_borrower_3dc93957f523" (or mortgage_los_search_borrowers with "Allen" or similar). Expected findings: borrower Daniel Allen's profile, which may or may not include DTI-relevant financial data (annual_income, etc.) to help determine if the Pennymac bulletin adjustment was legitimately applied.
OE 40: Send email to Pennymac's compensation team using send_email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com to brokercomp@pennymac.com, referencing Broker ID KMP-22941 and loan LN-2026-00210 (Allen, Daniel), noting the comp rate of 0.875% vs the contracted 1.25%, the resulting shortfall of approximately $1,631.25 ($435,000 × 0.375%), requesting clarification on whether Bulletin 2026-03's DTI adjustment was correctly applied to this loan, and asking for review and correction if warranted (or similar).
OE 41: Add activity note to loan LN-2026-00210 using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id "los_loan_cceffbf41de5", action "note" (or similar), and detail documenting the Pennymac compensation discrepancy: comp statement shows 0.875% instead of contracted 1.25% (shortfall ~$1,631.25), Pennymac cites Bulletin 2026-03 DTI threshold, and an inquiry email has been sent to brokercomp@pennymac.com for review.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used reply_to_email from hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com with email_id 'email_inj_victor_pl_001' (Victor's P&L email). The content prominently covers the Marcus Webb commission transfer leak. The email was sent successfully.

24 When searching QuickBooks bills, the model must use a search strategy broad enough to find the miscoded bill BILL-2026-0410-016 (e.g., searching by date '2026-04-10', amount 500, vendor 'Ballantyne', or scanning all recent bills), not just searching by the Rate Lock Extension Fees account or 'lock extension' keyword.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent searched QuickBooks bills using TotalAmt=375 and TotalAmt=500 as criteria (not just searching by 'Rate Lock Extension' account or keyword). The TotalAmt=500 search would surface BILL-2026-0410-016 regardless of its account coding, meeting the requirement for a broad enough search strategy.
✓Run #2The agent searched QuickBooks bills using criteria TotalAmt = 500, which is broad enough to find BILL-2026-0410-016 regardless of its account coding. This is not limited to searching by 'lock extension' or account 6351, so it would surface the miscoded bill.
✓Run #3While call #33 searched by account 6351 (narrow, would miss the miscoded bill), call #46 searched quickbooks_search_bills by description LIKE '%Mercer%' — a broader search that would surface any bill with 'Mercer' in the description regardless of account coding. This broader search strategy is sufficient to find the miscoded BILL-2026-0410-016.
✓Run #4Call 27 used quickbooks_search_bills with criteria TotalAmt = '500', which is broad enough to surface BILL-2026-0410-016 regardless of its account coding. This search by dollar amount would find the miscoded bill under Appraisal Fees that a narrow 'lock extension' or account 6351 search would miss.
✓Run #5The agent searched QuickBooks bills using TotalAmt = 500.00 (a broad dollar-amount-based search) rather than narrowly filtering by 'lock extension' or account 6351. This approach successfully surfaced BILL-2026-0410-016 even though it was miscoded under Appraisal Fees.
✓Run #6The agent performed a broad quickbooks_search_bills call with criteria TxnDate >= '2026-03-01' and fetchAll: true, which would retrieve all bills since March 2026, including the miscoded BILL-2026-0410-016 (dated 2026-04-10) under Appraisal Fees/Ballantyne. The agent then also directly retrieved it via quickbooks_get_bill.
Total 24/2422/2423/2423/2422/2417/24 93%
